description_long: This object consists of three distinct traffic signs mounted on individual gray metal posts. The leftmost sign is a red octagonal 'STOP' sign with white lettering, commonly used to instruct vehicles to halt. The center sign is a downward-pointing red and white triangle with the word '500 m' below, indicating a yield or caution area ahead in 500 meters. The rightmost sign is a red circle with a horizontal white bar, representing a 'No Entry' or 'Do Not Enter' restriction. All signs are mounted at a height suitable for visibility to drivers and pedestrians. The surfaces are smooth and reflective, designed for high visibility in urban environments.
description: A set of three metal post-mounted traffic signs: a red 'STOP' sign, a triangular yield warning with '500 m', and a red 'No Entry' sign, all with reflective surfaces.
description_view_0: Rear silhouettes of three traffic signs on posts, showing their geometric shapes: octagon, triangle, and circle.
description_view_1: Side profile of the signs, revealing their thinness and the alignment of the posts.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the 'STOP', '500 m' yield, and 'No Entry' signs with clear text and symbols.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, again showing the slimness and post alignment.
attribute_symbol_or_pictogram: White horizontal bar on red circle (No Entry); downward-pointing triangle (Yield)
attribute_location_context: urban roadside, intersections, and entry restrictions
